definition.name: archimedean_order_unit_space
definition.definition: An Archimedean order unit space is a triple (V, V^+, e) where V is a real vector space, V^+ ⊂ V is a proper cone (closed under addition and nonneg. scalar multiplication, with V^+ ∩ (-V^+) = {0}), and e ∈ V^+ is a distinguished element (the order unit) satisfying: for every v ∈ V there exists λ > 0 with -λe ≤ v ≤ λe (order unit property), and if v ≤ εe for all ε > 0 then v ≤ 0 (Archimedean property). The order unit norm is ||v|| = inf{λ > 0 : -λe ≤ v ≤ λe}. Every Archimedean order unit space embeds into a C(X) space by the Kadison representation theorem. The self-adjoint part A^{sa} of any unital C*-algebra is an Archimedean order unit space with order unit e = 1 and positive cone A^+ = {a*a : a ∈ A}. Reference: Alfsen-Shultz, State Spaces of Operator Algebras, §1.
